security._comment_csp: ws://127.0.0.1:* added so tauri-plugin-websocket can reach the r1 daemon on a loopback port chosen at runtime. Loopback only — explicitly NOT a ws: wildcard. Per spec desktop-cortex-augmentation §2.
security.csp: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: asset: https://asset.localhost; connect-src 'self' ipc: https://ipc.localhost ws://127.0.0.1:*

bundle.longDescription: Scaffold only. Full implementation: see desktop/PLAN.md and plans/work-orders/work-r1-desktop-app.md.
bundle._comment_externalBin: Bundles the r1 daemon as a sidecar fallback per spec desktop-cortex-augmentation §2 + §5. Tauri appends the host triple to the prefix at bundle time and resolves the matching file from desktop/src-tauri/binaries/. The five triples cover Linux x86_64, macOS arm64+x86_64, and Windows x86_64+arm64. Source binaries are produced by `desktop/scripts/copy-r1-binaries.sh` ahead of `cargo tauri build`.
